title: oxytropis viscida subsp. hudsonica
intro: Oxytropis viscida subsp. hudsonica, also known as the narrowleaf locoweed, is a perennial legume native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Oxytropis viscida subsp. hudsonica include its ability to treat digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, can be made by drying and steeping the leaves and stems, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
